title: Umami Dense Chinese Pilchards Dan Dan Mian
description: A savory Hakka Chinese noodle dish packed with umami flavor, featuring pilchards in a spicy bhuna sauce, served in a mason jar. This recipe is anti-inflammatory-friendly and perfect for a quick lunch.

ingredients.name: Pilchards
ingredients.notes: look for small, oily fish with a red stripe on the back

ingredients.name: Bhuna paste
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, contains mustard seeds, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, and chili peppers

ingredients.name: Noodles
ingredients.notes: look for Hakka-style noodles with a chewy texture

instructions: Heat sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add chili peppers and saut9 until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add garlic and ginger and saut9 until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add pilchards and cook until they release their oils and start to flake, about 3-4 minutes.
Add bhuna paste and stir-fry until combined with pilchards.
Add soy sauce and brown sugar and stir-fry until combined.
Add noodles and stir-fry until coated with sauce.
Transfer noodles to a mason jar and garnish with sesame seeds, green onions, and chili peppers.
Serve immediately, with additional chili peppers on the side if desired.
